name: UAC Bypass Attempt using Registry
description: Detects attempts to bypass User Account Control (UAC) by modifying specific registry keys associated with auto-elevated applications. Adversaries leverage these techniques to elevate process privileges on the system, often by manipulating `\mscfile\shell\open\command\` or `\ms-settings\shell\open\command\` paths. This is a critical indicator of privilege escalation.

prompts.prompt: A critical privilege escalation attempt has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**. The registry key **{target_object}**, a known target for UAC bypass exploits, was modified by the process **{image}** under the user **{user}**. This activity is a strong indicator of an adversary attempting to bypass User Account Control (**MITRE ATT&CK T1548.002**) to gain elevated privileges. The event ID is **{event_id}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the origin of the process and its full intent. If the modification is not part of a legitimate installation or system update, this is a confirmed security incident.

parameters.name: target_object
parameters.description: The full path of the registry key or value that was modified.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The process that performed the registry modification.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the modification.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon event ID for the registry modification (12, 13, or 14).

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
